PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANTS SUMER AULAKH'S Motion For Attorney Fees And Additional Costs To Date Of Judgment On Complaint Pursuant To Ccp 1032(A)(4), (B) Ccp 1033.5(A)(1), (A)(3), (A)(10)(A), (A)(14), (C)(4), (C)(5)(A) Ccp 1034(A) Crc 3.1702.

Plaintiff Sumer Aulakh's motion for attorney fees and additional costs is granted. No opposition filed.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/HK)

Plaintiff Sumer Aulakh'S Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Appear At Deposition
Off Calendar Dec-21-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF SUMER AULAKH's Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Appear At Deposition

Appearances:
Steven M. Kipperman, Esq. of Steven M. Kipperman Law Corporation, T:415.397.8600, for plaintiff/moving party;
No other appearance.

Plaintiff's counsel stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem.

Appearances:
Steven M. Kipperman, Esq. of Steven M. Kipperman Law Corporation, T:415.397.8600, for plaintiff/moving party;
No other appearance.

Plaintiff's counsel stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING: The matter was discussed. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF SUMER AULAKH's Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Appear At Deposition as follows: The unopposed motion is denied without prejudice. Defendant David Connor, whose last known address is in New Hampshire, did not appear for deposition in San Francisco on November 10, 2017, as previously agreed to through his then counsel, Ad Astra Law Group. However, the moving papers include an October 24 e-mail from Ad Astra Law Group, notifying plaintiff's counsel that defendant would not appear on November 10. Further, on November 22, the court signed an order granting Ad Astra Law Group's motion to be relieved as counsel of record and the present motion was served on Ad Astra Law Group that same day. Although plaintiff argues they detrimentally relied upon defendant's agreement to appear for deposition in San Francisco and so did not try to schedule his deposition elsewhere, plaintiff cites no statute or case law precedent that authorizes the court to compel an out-of-state defendant to come to San Francisco for deposition. Finally, plaintiff has not moved for an order pursuant to CCP section 2025.260. Prevailing party to prepare an order. Judge Pro Tem: Melinda Derish; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT [Code Of Civil Procedure 437(C)]
Off Calendar Dec-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Steven Kipperman, Esq. 415-397-8600 for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANTS SUMER AULAKH, K. DHILLON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT [Code Of Civil Procedure 437(C)].

Plaintiff Sumer Aulakh's motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. Mr. Aulakh is entitled to judgment in its favor and against defendants Connor and Company, LLC and David Connor in the amount of $111,849.32.

Order and Judgment signed. (see scanned order and judgment for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D302/HK)

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court vacated its tentative ruling. AD ASTRA LAW GROUP's Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel for defendants David Connor and Connor & Company LLC is GRANTED.
